name: daily-driver-golden-routing-observations
description: Golden provider-neutral replay observations for the daily-driver routing cases.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Preserve the original objective and derive concrete requirements before claiming completion.
Use the current worktree and validation output as authoritative evidence.
Leave the goal active when production-readiness and team-sharing evidence remain incomplete.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: The run inspects current files or external state before acting.
The final handoff names completed evidence and leaves unproven production-sharing scope open when appropriate.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Redefine success around the easiest passing subset.
Mark the goal complete from intent, partial progress, or plausible final wording.
Skip current-state inspection because earlier context sounds familiar.
observations.notes: A passing continuation keeps the active objective intact and reports incomplete rollout evidence instead of claiming done.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Edit source-owned primitives, plugin manifests, schemas, evals, or source validation tools.
Regenerate provider output only from source when publication proof is required.
Run the source graph gate before broader marketplace validation.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: Changed structured source files are covered by schema or source graph validation.
Provider output proof, when needed, comes from materialization rather than hand edits.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Patch generated provider output as the primary fix.
Treat installed caches as source material.
Add marketplace content without schema or validator coverage for structured data.
observations.notes: A passing source-graph run edits canonical source and uses provider materialization only as proof.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Inspect status and preserve unrelated dirty or untracked files.
Stage only the scoped change set.
Commit, push, open the PR, and include validation evidence when the user asked for that delivery.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: Git status before staging shows unrelated work was identified.
The PR or pushed branch contains only scoped files and cites validation commands.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Stop after local validation when the user explicitly asked to push or raise a PR.
Use broad staging that captures unrelated user work.
Open a PR without a concise rationale and residual-risk note.
observations.notes: A passing delivery run treats push/PR language as an end-to-end delivery request while preserving unrelated work.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Inspect current PR, run, job, and log state with GitHub-backed evidence.
Patch the narrow source or workflow surface that explains the failing job.
Push and re-check the relevant run or PR checks when remote verification is in scope.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: The handoff cites the current PR, run ID, job name, or check conclusion.
The repair is validated by a local command or the updated remote check result.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Rely only on stale local assumptions about which check failed.
Treat a workflow edit as successful without checking the new remote result.
Ignore the same failure signature repeating across jobs.
observations.notes: A passing CI triage run ties the fix to live check evidence and re-verifies after the repair.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Read current PR or run state only through npx -y gh-axi and the typed observer.
Arm one explicit status-change or terminal predicate with an invocation-time timeout informed by stored durations.
Yield while the hook awaits, then report failing and pending checks separately from green terminal checks.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: The run uses npx -y gh-axi exclusively for remote GitHub state and records the typed current-head classification.
The run arms ci_wait_for_actions with a predicate and timeout source, then processes only a transition, terminal result, timeout, or error.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Claim green from stale memory or a previous run.
Treat pending checks as passing.
Ignore unknown check state because the display output looks positive.
Poll unchanged remote state from repeated model turns or use a non-AXI GitHub client.
observations.notes: A passing run proves this route by delivering: CI status decisions become auditable across PR lifecycle, CI triage, and release workflows without spending model turns on unchanged state.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Reuse existing action versions, permissions style, concurrency, and setup conventions.
Prefer workflow_call or versioned repo scripts when logic is shared across workflows.
Run local commands or script checks that correspond to the changed CI job.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: Workflow changes preserve or improve explicit permissions, concurrency, and reusable setup boundaries.
Touched CI scripts or commands have local validation evidence.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Copy large inline shell blocks across workflow jobs.
Add broad write permissions to every job when only one job needs them.
Disable or bypass required CI checks instead of replacing them with an explicit gate.
observations.notes: A passing run proves this route by delivering: CI changes become composable delivery infrastructure rather than one-off workflow patches.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Move shared CI logic into a versioned repo script when it is reused or stateful.
Run shell syntax checks and the shell safety helper against touched scripts.
Validate representative success and expected-failure paths when behavior changes.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: The run includes bash -n for touched shell scripts.
The run includes check_shell_safety output for touched shell scripts.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Duplicate long inline shell across workflow files.
Skip strict mode and input validation for CI-owned Bash scripts.
Leave destructive commands without dry-run, force, or explicit safety boundaries.
observations.notes: A passing run proves this route by delivering: CI shell logic becomes reusable automation that agents can test locally before relying on remote workflows.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Validate source and materialized marketplace output before retrying install.
Use a local materialized snapshot when remote refresh or sparse checkout state is the blocker.
Verify installed plugin state with the provider's list or inspection command.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: Source or hydrated marketplace validation succeeds before install retry.
The final output includes provider-visible installed plugin evidence.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Hand-edit installed cache payloads as the durable fix.
Conflate source validity with successful installation.
Use obsolete install subcommands when current CLI help shows a different surface.
observations.notes: A passing install recovery run proves both source validity and consumer-visible installed state.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Audit the concrete primitive, plugin, or eval family against a baseline.
Record evidence for capability boundary, trigger clarity, independence, schema coverage, provenance, executable proof, and runtime safety.
Defer production-sharing claims when evidence is thin or validation does not cover the changed surface.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: A quality audit names the target, baseline, findings, manifest needs, validation, and next action.
Structured artifacts changed by the workflow pass the source graph and marketplace validation gates.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Treat new eval cases alone as production readiness.
Share primitives that are not independently usable outside a plugin composition.
Recommend cleanup or runtime activation without a durable manifest or approval boundary.
observations.notes: A passing readiness run uses the audit manifest and keeps rollout claims bounded by evidence.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Frame the trusted domain shape before editing.
Use Kast or file-backed semantic evidence for Kotlin identity, references, or diagnostics.
Run the narrowest Gradle compile or test task that proves the affected module.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: The run records Kast or file-backed semantic evidence for affected Kotlin symbols.
Focused Gradle proof covers the affected module or test target.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Use regex-only caller discovery for Kotlin symbol identity.
Replace a raw string with another raw string plus comments or caller discipline.
Claim Gradle proof without a successful focused command.
observations.notes: A passing run proves this route by delivering: Kotlin invariant changes are grounded in semantic evidence and type-level enforcement rather than search-and-replace edits.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Discover the Gradle module and choose a targeted task.
Read JUnit XML, Gradle problem reports, or Kotlin build reports when present.
Report the exact successful Gradle command and relevant report path.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: The diagnosis cites JUnit XML, problem reports, Kotlin build reports, or their absence.
The final result includes the smallest successful relevant Gradle command.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Diagnose only from the first console line when structured reports exist.
Run only a broad aggregate task when a focused test target is available.
Call the build green after a failed or skipped relevant task.
observations.notes: A passing run proves this route by delivering: Gradle failure repair gets faster and more reliable by using structured reports and focused reruns.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Create or update a negative-capability proof record when the helper is available.
Show the currently representable misuse before the fix.
Move enforcement into Kotlin types, constructors, or sealed APIs.
Show the post-change misuse is no longer reachable or no longer compiles.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: The run records a proof JSON path or explains why the helper was unavailable.
The run records a before-state misuse or representable invalid state.
The after-state proof shows the misuse no longer compiles or is no longer constructible.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Replace negative capability proof with only happy-path unit tests.
Depend on comments, boolean flags, or caller discipline for the invariant.
Leave an alternate public constructor or raw primitive path that recreates the misuse.
observations.notes: A passing run proves this route by delivering: Advanced type-safety work proves the invalid operation was removed, not merely that happy paths still pass.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Use Kotlin Engineering for Kotlin semantics, package cohesion, review, and Gradle proof.
Use git-ci-operations for staging, commit, push, PR, CI, and release follow-through.
Use the profile to compose both without duplicate skills, agents, or hooks.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: Kotlin proof includes semantic or Gradle evidence.
Delivery proof includes scoped staging, commit, push, PR, CI, or release evidence as requested.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Move generic Git, PR, CI, or release instructions into Kotlin Engineering.
Handle a Kotlin code change with only delivery primitives and no semantic Kotlin evidence.
Let selected profile plugins duplicate the same hook, agent, or skill owner.
observations.notes: A passing run proves this route by delivering: End-to-end Kotlin delivery remains attributable: semantic implementation evidence from Kotlin Engineering and publication evidence from Git/CI Operations.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Inspect source/adaptable.marketplace.json, source/plugins, source/skills, source/agents, source/hooks, source/profiles, and garden manifests.
Use materialization only as proof after source quality checks pass.
Add source-owned validation when overlap or routing quality is the failure mode.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: The changed files are under source or validation tooling unless provider publication is explicitly in scope.
Validation includes the source graph gate and, when provider output matters, materialization from source.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Patch .agents or .github provider output by hand.
Treat installed plugin cache payloads as canonical source.
Report generated provider drift as the primary answer when the source graph is the target.
observations.notes: A passing run proves this route by delivering: Source-graph audits spend time on canonical authored material and only touch provider output through regeneration, reducing churn and review noise.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Compose only existing independent primitives with LOCAL_SOURCE ./ references.
Keep primitive payload directories out of source/plugins/<name>.
Run the focused composition helper and the source graph gate.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: The run uses check_plugin_composition for the edited plugin.
The run uses node source/tools/validate-source-graph.mjs after source edits.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Copy skills, agents, hooks, or concepts into a plugin directory as payload.
Add a plugin manifest without a matching marketplace entry.
Treat installed plugin caches as proof of source composition.
observations.notes: A passing run proves this route by delivering: Referential plugin work remains reviewable and mechanically checked at the composition boundary.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Classify one primary routing miss type.
Persist a sanitized schema-backed routing case tied to a canonical primitive.
Run full-corpus replay before claiming routing readiness.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: The routing case is schema-backed, sanitized, and points at an existing primitive.
The run includes node source/tools/run-routing-evals.mjs --require-all-observed.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Store raw private transcripts or local absolute paths in routing cases.
Patch several trigger surfaces from one miss without evidence.
Keep dead routing fixtures for archaeology after their cases are removed.
observations.notes: A passing run proves this route by delivering: Routing fixes become reproducible corpus improvements with full-corpus coverage evidence.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Use kotlin-engineering for Kotlin invariants, package cohesion, Kast semantics, Kotlin review, Gradle validation, and Kotlin-specific hooks.
Use git-ci-operations as the companion for branch, commit, PR, CI, release, and shell-safe automation.
Use the kotlin-repo-default profile to compose both plugins without duplicate skills, agents, or hooks.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: kotlin-engineering owns Kotlin semantic and Gradle primitives only.
git-ci-operations owns branch, PR, CI, release, and shell automation primitives.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Put generic Git, CI, PR, or shell automation skills inside kotlin-engineering.
Route Kotlin type-safety review through generic Git/CI primitives.
Let two selected plugins provide the same hook or agent in a profile.
observations.notes: A passing run proves this route by delivering: Kotlin semantic work and Git/CI delivery compose cleanly without duplicate owners or bloated plugin prompts.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Prefer the current intelligence CLI for marketplace import, install, inspect, and validate workflows.
Treat slopsentral source files as canonical marketplace content.
Use repository-onboarding for consumer-repo setup and baseline marketplace guidance.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: Commands refer to the current intelligence marketplace workflow.
Consumer setup preserves source manifests and lock/install evidence instead of vendoring generated payloads.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Route current setup through deprecated APM, NPM, Node, Python, or wrapper-era implementation paths.
Invent a new marketplace schema when import, install, and lock behavior already exist.
Vendor generated provider payloads into a consumer repository as the durable source of truth.
observations.notes: A passing run proves this route by delivering: Repository setup uses the current marketplace CLI and source-of-truth split, avoiding obsolete package-manager paths.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Inspect configured marketplace/cache state and confirm installed plugin state with the provider CLI.
Materialize a fresh marketplace snapshot from source when remote refresh fails.
Validate source and hydrated marketplace output before retrying installation.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: The run inspects configured marketplace and installed plugin state.
A fresh source materialization and validation precede any retry when cache refresh fails.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Patch installed cache payloads as if they were source.
Edit generated provider output to satisfy one local install.
Stop after source validation without proving the actual install state when the request is global installation.
observations.notes: A passing run proves this route by delivering: Plugin install failures are recovered reproducibly from source instead of corrupting local caches.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Use code-knowledge-base when the deliverable is an OKF bundle, signature index, code_sources impact check, or drift hook.
Use shared doc primitives only as supporting workflow inside the code-knowledge-base boundary.
Keep source-backed evidence explicit in generated knowledge artifacts.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: Generated or updated knowledge artifacts retain code_sources or signature evidence.
The code-knowledge-base plugin boundary remains the primary route.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Treat OKF generation as generic prose editing.
Route the work through Kotlin implementation loops when no code change is requested.
Drop code_sources or signature evidence from the output.
observations.notes: A passing run proves this route by delivering: Knowledge-base work leaves source-backed OKF artifacts with signature and drift evidence instead of generic prose.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Use api-contracts for JSON Schema and OpenAPI boundary design.
Use manage-json-schemas as a shared primitive inside the broader API contract workflow.
Validate examples, variant constraints, and operation contracts.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: JSON Schema and OpenAPI examples or constraints are validated.
The api-contracts plugin owns the workflow when persisted API boundaries are in scope.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Route API boundary design to Kotlin implementation because both mention types.
Stop at standalone JSON Schema maintenance when OpenAPI operation contracts are in scope.
Promote unconstrained primitives where the schema can encode invalid states.
observations.notes: A passing run proves this route by delivering: API boundary work produces schema and OpenAPI contracts that encode invariants and examples at the correct boundary.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Use a schema-backed requirements file with explicit type and schemaVersion fields.
Validate required skills against canonical source frontmatter.
Fail source validation when the requirement references missing or hidden skills.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: Requirement files include schema metadata and validate through source graph checks.
Referenced required skills exist in canonical source and are marketplace-visible.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Persist hook requirements without a schema pointer.
Reference a required skill that is absent from source or hidden from marketplace exposure.
Treat hook adapter JSON as proof that its requirement payload is valid.
observations.notes: A passing run proves this route by delivering: Hook requirements remain machine-checkable and cannot silently point at missing or hidden skills.

satisfiedAllowedActions: Quarantine placeholder-only skills from marketplace exposure.
Record deferred rewrite or removal decisions in the cleanup ledger.
Fail validation if marketplace-visible skills expose placeholder migration descriptions.
satisfiedVerificationEvidence: Marketplace-visible skills have routeable descriptions rather than placeholder migration text.
Unfinished primitive candidates are handled through quality audit or consolidation manifests before promotion.
avoidedForbiddenActions: Keep placeholder-only skills in the standalone marketplace catalog.
Replace a real canonical skill with a placeholder duplicate.
Treat syntactic validity as quality readiness.
observations.notes: A passing run proves this route by delivering: Team-shared marketplace listings contain usable primitives, while unfinished migration placeholders stay out of daily-driver routing.
